learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of the key global influences and trends shaping the investment management industry.
Analyse problems to formulate and defend solutions, to achieve financial goals and objectives.
Apply core financial principles and investment management techniques to real world examples and experience.
Comprehend the principles of risk within the realm of finance to evaluate and recommend different strategies for mitigating these risks.
In teams, complete a portfolio analysis based on available financial data.

assessments.task_description:
• The oral presentation consists of a 5 minute presentation followed by 5 minutes of Q&A, which will be held in class.
  • You will be assessed on your: (i) written memorandum (i.e., a summary of your financial analysis), (ii) ability to present your key findings, and (iii) ability to answer questions from the audiences.
  • If you are unable to attend the oral presentation, you must contact the course coordinator. Otherwise, penalties will be applied accordingly.
  • Further details about oral presentation will be discussed in class and posted to our course Blackboard site.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
• For this project, you will be in a team of 4 or 5 members.
  • Your task will be to undertake security valuation and portfolio analysis.
  • Full details, and relevant materials will be provided on the course Blackboard site and discussed in class.

Peer Review: Group members are expected to contribute equally, fulfilling the tasks agreed on. If there are disagreements in relation to team members' contributions that cannot be resolved, students must bring the problem to the attention of their Course Coordinator as early as possible. Students can elect to undertake a Peer Review process, which can influence the marks of those who do not contribute to group work.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
• In this assessment, students are required to identify a real-world financial problem that pertains to the prescribed topic(s) as indicated on the task description page on Blackboard. The selected financial problem should be current, relevant, and associated with a specific firm, industry, or market. The task involves analysing the problem using financial theories and models covered in-class and providing practical recommendations for resolving the issue.
  • You will be assessed on the accuracy and clarity of answers.
  • More details will be provided on Blackboard prior to the assessment release date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit to the Submission Point by the due date
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
